title: SEC settlement with Delphia over false and misleading statements about AI and machine learning use
summary: The SEC found that Delphia made false and misleading statements in filings, advertisements, and social media about using artificial intelligence and machine learning with client data in its investment process.

source_claims.claim: The SEC announced settled charges against Delphia for making false and misleading statements about its purported use of AI and machine learning.

The SEC order states that from at least August 2019 to August 2023 Delphia represented that it used AI and machine learning to analyze retail clients' spending and social media data when no such data was being used in its investment process.

legal_normative_notes: Use as an SEC enforcement finding and settlement record, not as a judicial determination of broader fraud facts.
Good paper-grade example of AI capability exaggeration in regulated financial advice marketing.

review_notes: Authoritative single-regulator package; no independent corroboration added this cycle because the sprint favored exact authoritative support over breadth.

title: SEC settlement with Global Predictions over false and misleading AI financial-adviser claims
summary: The SEC found that Global Predictions made false and misleading claims about its use of AI, including describing itself as the first regulated AI financial advisor and misrepresenting certain service features.

source_claims.claim: The SEC announced settled charges against Global Predictions for false and misleading statements about its purported use of AI.
The SEC order states that Global Predictions made false and misleading claims about its use of AI, its status as the first regulated AI financial advisor, and the services it offered.

legal_normative_notes: Use as an SEC enforcement finding and settlement record, not as proof that all AI-enabled advisory products are similarly misleading.
Useful comparator case for paper tables on AI washing in regulated sectors.

review_notes: Authoritative single-regulator package; further crosswalking to AIID/OECD/AVID remains open.
